steps.title: Identify where the independent clauses meet
steps.content: At the blank, the text transitions from one independent clause (ending with "temple walls") to another (beginning with "he discovered the markings...").

steps.title: Apply the independent-clause separation rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be separated by terminal punctuation (a period) or a semicolon, not by a comma alone or no punctuation at all. Choice D — walls. With provides the period.
  • A. walls, with — comma splice; a comma cannot join two independent clauses.
  • B. walls with — run-on; no punctuation between two independent clauses.
  • C. walls so with — ungrammatical; "so" does not function as a clause separator here.
  • D. walls. With — correctly terminates the first clause with a period.
